nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; tightly packed schools along reef margins of rocky shorelines, just below breaking surf; young found in tide pools. Also inhabits estuaries and coastal waters.

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on free-swimming crustaceans and small fish in the wild, primarily at night. In captivity, generally accepts mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, frozen meaty foods, flake, and pellet foods. Ghost shrimp have been reported to be useful for encouraging initial feeding.

avoidWith: small fish that could be preyed upon

summary: The Flagtail Grunt is a silver, active schooling fish recognizable by its deeply forked tail fin marked with alternating black and white bands. It tends to do well in larger reef aquariums with open swimming space and is generally considered an intermediate species to keep.
careNotes:
The Flagtail Grunt (Kuhlia mugil) is a reef-margin schooling fish from the broad Indo-Pacific, typically found just below the surf line along rocky shores. In captivity, keeping a full school is generally considered impractical given the space demands of a fish that tends to reach 25–40 cm at maturity; most aquarists keep one specimen or a small group in a large aquarium. The species tends to be a nocturnal feeder in the wild but has been reported to adjust to daytime feeding in aquaria. Initial feeding may require live ghost shrimp before transitioning to frozen or prepared foods.

Temperament reports vary somewhat across sources: the majority of references rate the Flagtail Grunt as semi-aggressive, while a minority characterize it as peaceful. The semi-aggressive assessment is the more widely cited position, and observed aggression tends to be directed at smaller tankmates rather than at same-size or larger companions.

Aquarium temperature is generally advised in the 22–27 °C range for home keeping, though wild specimens have been recorded in warmer waters. If the fish reaches its full wild maximum of 40 cm, a tank of 1,500 L or more becomes appropriate to provide adequate swimming space.

The Flagtail Grunt has no venomous spines and no documented sex-change behavior in aquarium literature. Its IUCN conservation status is Least Concern.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has been reported as possible, but offspring numbers are insufficient for commercial demand. No detailed captive-breeding protocols have been documented in the available aquarium literature.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy, native range, maximum size (40 cm SL), and temperature range (24.5–29.3 °C from wild data). FishBase maximum significantly exceeds the common aquarium trade size.

additional.name: FishyWiki
additional.notes: Confirmed care level (moderate), minimum tank (680 L), salinity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), temperament (semi-aggressive), and reef safety. Used in tank-volume average.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Provided lifespan estimate (5–10 years) and additional diet notes. Rated temperament as peaceful (minority view; semi-aggressive adopted). Tank minimum cited as 150 gal / 568 L; included in average.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Confirmed maximum size of 40 cm and temperature range (23–27 °C). Recommended 1,500 L tank based on full adult size; this outlier was noted in careNotes but not included in the minimum-volume average used for a typical home-aquarium context.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding has been reported as possible but is not commercially viable. No detailed captive-breeding protocols have been documented in the available aquarium literature.
